items.title: Asylum seekers in tears as Trump suspends Biden-era app | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Shortly after President Donald Trump inauguration, the US border authorities said they had shut down Joe Biden's CBP One entry program, which had allowed hundreds of thousands of migrants to enter the US legally by scheduling an appointment on an app
#News #Reuters #Newsfeed #DonaldTrump #Migration

items.title: Trump declares energy emergency, vows to boost US fossil fuels | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>President Donald Trump laid out a sweeping plan to boost oil and gas production, including by declaring a national energy emergency to speed permitting, rolling back environmental protections, and withdrawing the US from the Paris climate treaty.

items.title: Donald Trump pardons Jan. 6 defendants from redecorated Oval Office | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>President Donald Trump pardoned nearly everyone criminally charged with participating in the Jan. 6, 2021, attack on the US Capitol, in a show of solidarity with supporters who stormed the seat of American power in his name.

items.title: LIVE: Outside Washington, D.C., jail after Trump pardons nearly all charged in US Capitol riot
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>The view from outside a jail in Washington, D.C., after President Donald Trump pardoned nearly everyone criminally charged with participating in the Jan. 6, 2021, attack on the U.S. Capitol, in a show of solidarity with supporters who stormed the seat of American power in his name. Some federal inmates serving Jan. 6-related sentences could be released as soon as Monday, a Bureau of Prisons spokesperson said.
 
#Live #Trump #DonaldTrump #Jan6 #pardon #inauguration #ExecutiveOrder #jail #prison #WashingtonDC #politics #news 

Keep up with the latest news from around the world: https://www.reuters.com/</article>

items.title: Melania Trump 'transformed' her 'understated' hat, says designer | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>As President Donald Trump entered the US Capitol Rotunda to be sworn into office, he moved to kiss the cheek of his wife Melania Trump but was blocked by an ever-present, wide-brimmed hat that the First Lady wore throughout the entirety of Inauguration Day.
